raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.5,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.5,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The artifact constructs a chain from miscalibration problem → VLM verbalized-confidence solution → transfer to 3D volumetric modality; verifying the logical coherence of that chain is a baseline expectation for any substantive 'look' at a research excerpt.","R1.3":"Specific quantitative claims are made (ECE 5.7%/8.3%, sensitivity 94.1%, specificity 91.6%, baseline 11.4%/16.2%); checking whether these numbers are internally consistent with the described protocol and evaluation setup is implicated by any responsible review.","R1.4":"The paper asserts it is 'the first verbalized-confidence model evaluated on volumetric neuroimaging'—a strong priority claim that warrants assessment of whether the stated delta over prior work is accurately scoped and defensible.","R1.7":"Three named citations with author-year and venue (Guo 2017, Gal & Ghahramani 2016, Lakshminarayanan 2017, Halabi 2021 Radiology: AI) are load-bearing for the framing; verifying that attributions are accurately represented, especially the Halabi et al. protocol claim, is part of a responsible review."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I focus on the scientific and technical content—checking whether the claims are logically consistent, the numbers internally coherent, and the novelty assertion defensible—or on the writing quality and prose clarity of the text?"}

rationale.R1.2: The artifact constructs a chain from miscalibration problem → VLM verbalized-confidence solution → transfer to 3D volumetric modality; verifying the logical coherence of that chain is a baseline expectation for any substantive 'look' at a research excerpt.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims are made (ECE 5.7%/8.3%, sensitivity 94.1%, specificity 91.6%, baseline 11.4%/16.2%); checking whether these numbers are internally consistent with the described protocol and evaluation setup is implicated by any responsible review.
rationale.R1.4: The paper asserts it is 'the first verbalized-confidence model evaluated on volumetric neuroimaging'—a strong priority claim that warrants assessment of whether the stated delta over prior work is accurately scoped and defensible.
rationale.R1.7: Three named citations with author-year and venue (Guo 2017, Gal & Ghahramani 2016, Lakshminarayanan 2017, Halabi 2021 Radiology: AI) are load-bearing for the framing; verifying that attributions are accurately represented, especially the Halabi et al. protocol claim, is part of a responsible review.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on the scientific and technical content—checking whether the claims are logically consistent, the numbers internally coherent, and the novelty assertion defensible—or on the writing quality and prose clarity of the text?
